title: Remove a Tote From the System
summary: Use the documented tote management function shown in the training source to remove a specified tote from the system. The source confirms that a Remove Tote function exists on the tote management API page and that it removes a specified tote from the system, but it does not provide exact field names, button labels beyond the function name, or confirmation message details.
when_to_use: Use when support needs to remove a specified tote from the system using the documented tote management function shown in the training material.
not_for: Do not use when the tote cannot be uniquely identified for removal.
Do not use as a substitute for undocumented recovery or rollback actions, because the source does not provide those details.

responsibilities: Identify the tote to be removed.
Access the tote management interface or API-backed remove function.
Submit the remove action and verify the tote no longer appears active if a status view or system response is available.

safety_notes: Use only the documented Remove Tote function shown in the source.
The source does not provide rollback steps or detailed confirmation behavior; escalate if the result is unclear.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to tote management interface or API-backed remove function

access_required: Access to tote management interface or API-backed remove function
system_preconditions: A specific tote has been identified for removal.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the tote management API features, including Remove Tote.

steps.title: Identify the tote to remove
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct tote is targeted before using the remove function.
steps.instruction: Identify the specific tote that needs to be removed from the system using the available tote identifier.
steps.expected_result: A single specified tote is selected for removal.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be uniquely identified or there is uncertainty about which tote should be removed.

actions.label: Identify tote
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote that needs to be removed from the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be uniquely identified for removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Remove Tote — Removes a specified tote from the system.

steps.title: Open the Remove Tote function
steps.purpose: Navigate to the documented tote management function used for tote removal.
steps.instruction: Open the tote management function that includes the Remove Tote option on the API page shown in the training material.
steps.expected_result: The Remove Tote function is available for use.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote management page or interface shows Remove Tote as an available function.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove Tote function is not visible or the operator cannot access the tote management page.

actions.label: Open remove function
actions.exact_action: Open the tote management function for removing a tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page showing Remove Tote among the available tote management features.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Remove Tote function is not available in the interface.
Required access to the tote management interface is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Aveva API page lists API Features for Tote Management: Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

steps.title: Enter or select the tote
steps.purpose: Provide the specified tote to the remove function.
steps.instruction: Enter or select the specified tote to remove using the available interface.
steps.expected_result: The intended tote is populated in the remove workflow.
steps.healthy_condition: The interface reflects the specified tote selected for removal.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be entered, selected, or confirmed in the remove workflow.

actions.label: Specify tote
actions.exact_action: Enter or select the specified tote to remove.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page where Remove Tote is available; use the interface area associated with tote management to specify the tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: The specified tote cannot be selected or entered.
There is uncertainty that the selected tote is the correct tote.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Remove Tote — Removes a specified tote from the system.

steps.title: Submit the remove action
steps.purpose: Execute the documented removal of the specified tote from the system.
steps.instruction: Submit the remove action through the available interface.
steps.expected_result: The system accepts the remove request for the specified tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The remove action completes through the interface without an obvious error.
steps.failure_condition: The remove action does not complete or the interface indicates a problem.

actions.label: Submit removal
actions.exact_action: Submit the remove action through the available interface.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page showing that Remove Tote is a supported function.

stop_or_escalate_if: The remove action does not complete.
The interface shows an error or does not provide a clear completion state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Remove Tote — Removes a specified tote from the system.

steps.title: Verify the tote is removed
steps.purpose: Confirm that the requested tote removal took effect.
steps.instruction: Verify through the available tote status view or system response that the tote has been removed from the system.
steps.expected_result: The specified tote no longer appears active in the system response or available tote status view.
steps.healthy_condition: The system indicates the tote has been removed or no longer appears active.
steps.failure_condition: The tote still appears active afterward or the result cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Verify removal
actions.exact_action: Verify through the available tote status view or system response that the tote has been removed from the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API feature set, including Get Tote Task, which indicates related tote status/query capability exists in the same area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote still appears active afterward.
The source-provided interface does not provide a clear confirmation outcome.
Confirmation messages or rollback steps are needed but not provided by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Remove Tote — Removes a specified tote from the system.
Aveva API page lists API Features for Tote Management: Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

success_criteria: The specified tote is removed from the system.
The tote no longer appears active in the available tote status view or system response, if available.
healthy_conditions: The correct tote is uniquely identified before removal.
The Remove Tote function is available on the tote management API page.
The remove action completes without an obvious interface error.
The tote no longer appears active after removal.
failure_conditions: The tote cannot be uniquely identified for removal.
The Remove Tote function cannot be accessed.
The remove action does not complete.
The tote still appears active afterward.
The source does not provide exact confirmation messages or rollback steps.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the tote cannot be uniquely identified for removal.
Escalate if the remove action does not complete or the tote still appears active afterward.
Escalate if confirmation of removal is required but the interface response is unclear, because the source does not provide exact confirmation messages or rollback steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide showing the tote management API features, including Remove Tote.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: OCR: Remove Tote — Removes a specified tote from the system.

missing_details: Exact navigation path to the tote management page is not provided in the source.
Exact UI field names and button labels for Remove Tote are not provided in the source.
Exact confirmation messages, error messages, and rollback steps are not provided in the source.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not specify whether additional approvals or role boundaries beyond support access are required.
